name: LITTLEBURN BH2A HUTTON SEAM MINE WATER BOREHOLE
units: metres, transcribed verbatim from typed summary log — convert ONLY downstream

annotations: typed Comments column: 'Borehole drilled to 43.5m, casing and hole grouted and re-drill through grout'
text clearly legible; class unknown because no strata were logged 0.00-45.20
daily drilling record 27/3/98 during redrill: hit cement at 24.00, Cement to 43.50, 'Sand and Grout Soft Damp' to 45.20 (grout/backfill, not natural strata)
typed Comments column (cell spans 45.20-50.00): 'Borehole cased and grouted and redrilled to 50m'
daily record 27/3/98: 'Sandstone Damp Poor Air Return's 47.50'
daily record 27/3/98: 'Mudstone Wet 50.00'; engineer then had hole grouted again (28/3/98) and redrilled through cement (3/4/98, 'Cement Hard Dry 50.00')
typed Comments column (cell spans 50.00-67.00): '2inch plastic pipe to 67m, with bottom 10m being slotted'
seam not named in original; 0.60 m coal above the Hutton workings horizon at 64.90

intervals.verbatim: Soft mudstone (damp) poor returns (Hutton mine workings)
annotations: Hutton mine workings named in original — worked-seam horizon, logged material is soft mudstone (collapse/goaf fill), not coal
daily record 3/4/98: 'Soft Mudstone Damp Poor Air Returns 65.34'
text clearly legible; 'Solid' is a driller's term with no lithology given and no air returns — cannot classify
daily record 6/4/98: 'Clean Out B/H To 66.00m', 'Sandstone Wet 67.00'

seam_marks.how: named in original: Soft mudstone (damp) poor returns (Hutton mine workings)

notes: Page 1 is a typed metric summary log (Littleburn BH2A (Hutton Seam BH), co-ords 425530mE 540242mN, drilled March 1998 to 17th April 1998) with Depth From/Depth To/Geology/Comments columns; it is the strata record transcribed above (thickness_m computed as To minus From; the sheet gives from/to depths, no thickness column, and all pairs are continuous with no gaps or arithmetic slips). Pages 2-6 are handwritten C.H. Steavenson DAILY DRILLING RECORD sheets (rig Gryphon, site Meadowfield/Medowfield) dated 27/3/98, 28/3/98, 3/4/98, 4/4/98 and 6/4/98; they are operational records (grouting, casing, pipe installation) whose lithology depths (47.50, 50.00, 53.94, 54.54, 56.94, 61.14, 64.90, 65.34, 66.00, 67.00) agree exactly with the typed log — no disagreements. Hole peculiarities from the daily records: original hole drilled to 43.5m then casing and hole grouted and redrilled through grout (hit cement at 24.00m, cement to 43.50, sand and grout to 45.20 on 27/3/98); hole grouted AGAIN above 50m on 28/3/98 (20x25kg bags cement, 60 gallons water, grout pushing water out of B/H after 15 bags) and redrilled 3/4/98 (wet grout at 7.00m to 25.00, damp grout to 41.00, cement hard dry to 50.00); 2-inch plastic pipe initially stopped at 57.00m (4/4/98), hole cleaned out and pipe lowered to 67.00m with bottom 10.00m slotted (57.00m of plain push cap on top), 10 bags pea gravel down hole, engineer wants 2 bags bentonite put down at later date (6/4/98, drilling complete); 16.00m of 8-inch casing grouted into B/H unable to pull out. No Ground Level (m AOD) box on any page — surface OD absent. No pencil marginalia. No Reduced Level column.
